questions.prompt: Which DMARC policy values are valid? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: DMARC defines exactly three policy values: none (report only), quarantine (deliver to spam), and reject. 'allow' and 'block' are not in the spec.

questions.prompt: A customer's DMARC is at p=none. They're not seeing spoofing rejected. What's the next move?
choices: Confirm SPF and DKIM are passing for legitimate senders, then move to p=quarantine, watch reports, then move to p=reject
Switch to p=block to enforce DMARC
Add ~all to the SPF record to enable enforcement
questions.explanation: p=none is the report-only stage. To actually enforce, you need quarantine (then reject), but only after verifying every legitimate sender passes alignment. Moving to reject without checking reports breaks legitimate mail.
